prompt: When can I have this?
comeback: Month 13.

prompt: Can we ship it Friday?
comeback: We can ship something Friday.

prompt: Can we get a demo-ready version by Monday?
comeback: We can get a Monday-ready version of the demo.

prompt: Is it done?
comeback: It's 'done' the way my gym membership is 'active.'

prompt: Can you prioritise it?
comeback: Sure, I'll put it right between 'never' and 'lol no.'

prompt: Can we fast-track it?
comeback: Sure, I'll put a little rocket emoji in the ticket.

prompt: Can we fast-track it?
comeback: We do have a fast lane. It merges back into the regular backlog about two miles later.

prompt: Can we fast-track it?
comeback: Absolutely, as soon as we fast-track the fast-track from yesterday.

prompt: Can we fast-track it?
comeback: Sure. Sales-fast or 'has actually been estimated by Engineering' fast?

prompt: Is this a priority?
comeback: It's our absolute top priority, right next to other top priorities.

prompt: The customer is threatening to churn.
comeback: Bold of them to assume we had a retention strategy.

prompt: The customer is threatening to churn.
comeback: So is my will to live, but we don't have a Jira ticket for that yet either.

prompt: I need this yesterday.
comeback: Let me check if we have any yesterdays left.

prompt: Can we make an exception?
comeback: The next exception reset is beginning of the next month.

prompt: What's the timeline?
comeback: What year works for you?

prompt: The customer is unhappy.
comeback: Join the queue. We provide sandwiches.

prompt: Just add it to the roadmap.
comeback: Consider it added.

prompt: Just add it to the roadmap.
comeback: Done. It now lives on the roadmap the way Catalonia lives in Spain.

prompt: So it's basically built, right?
comeback: Basically.

prompt: It's basically done, right?
comeback: It's done the way that AI has generated an answer - All good as long as you don't check.

prompt: The customer needs this to close the deal.
comeback: Funny, Engineering also needs things to close their tickets. We should introduce them.

prompt: The customer is still waiting for this feature.
comeback: Such patience. I respect it.

prompt: Just say yes and we'll figure it out later.
comeback: That's how we got the  three things we're currently un-figuring-out.

prompt: Why is it delayed again?
comeback: We're Agile, which means we can be behind schedule in two-week increments instead of one long disappointment.

prompt: Is it on the roadmap?
comeback: It's so far out on the roadmap it needs its own passport.

prompt: Steve said it's simple.
comeback: Steve also thinks Q3 has 5 months.

prompt: The customer says it's a quick fix.
comeback: Everyone's a senior engineer until the ticket's assigned to them.

prompt: Steve says it'll only take a day.
comeback: Steve also said he writes code in Japanese.

prompt: Is the timeline accurate?
comeback: It's accurate the way a fortune cookie is accurate.

prompt: Didn't we already decide this?
comeback: We did. That was the old strategy. Keep up.

prompt: Why do PMs talk like this?
comeback: Because crying doesn't fit in a Jira ticket.

prompt: Why are PMs like this?
comeback: Because Jira doesn't have a field for 'I want to lie down.'

prompt: Can we add just one more thing?
comeback: Sure, that's what MVP stands for — Massively Varying Promises.

prompt: Sales says the roadmap doesn't reflect customer needs.
comeback: Funny, the roadmap says the same thing about Sales' promises.

prompt: Are we aligned on this?
comeback: Completely. Sales is aligned with the customer. Product is aligned with reality.

prompt: Let's get everyone aligned on scope.
comeback: They're perfectly aligned the way two people are aligned in a tug-of-war.

prompt: Can we get everyone on the same page?
comeback: We're on the same book.

prompt: Get me alignment by EOD.
comeback: I can get you alignment or I can get you EOD. They don't travel together.

prompt: Are we aligned on what caused the issue?
comeback: Everyone agrees it's Product's fault, which is the fastest consensus this org has ever reached.

prompt: How do we stop this from happening again?
comeback: Easy. Next time, blame Product before the meeting instead of during it. Saves everyone 20 mins.

prompt: Do you ever say yes?
comeback: Yes.

prompt: Just give me a rough number.
comeback: Rough like the estimate, or rough like how this conversation is going to go?

prompt: It's basically already built, right?
comeback: It's basically built the way a pile of lumber is basically a house.

prompt: Can we get a status update?
comeback: Status: updating.

prompt: How's the sprint going?
comeback: Great, if you don't count the sprint goal.

prompt: I want to escalate this.
comeback: Sure, I'll move it from the pile to the taller pile.

prompt: This is an important, we need this fixed now.
comeback: Good to know we are an org of exclusively VIPs.

prompt: Can you explain to the customer why this is delayed
comeback: I can, but 'it's delayed because we have to building everything' wouldn't go well on a call.

prompt: The customer says they were promised this in the sales demo.
comeback: The demo promised a lot of things. So does the bus time table.

prompt: The customer wants an escalation call with Product?
comeback: Sure, I'll bring helmet and ballistic vest.

prompt: The customer said our competitor already has this.
comeback: Cool, send the competitor's PM congratulations.

prompt: What's blocking this?
comeback: Mostly the last thing that was blocking it.

prompt: Let's circle back on this.
comeback: Sure, I'll add it to the roundabout of what we've discussed before.

prompt: The customer says trust is broken.
comeback: Good, now it matches how Engineering's felt about me since last quarter.

prompt: The customer's threatening to sue.
comeback: Tell them they are not my first or last.

prompt: Can you at least give the customer some hope?
comeback: I can give them a ticket number.

prompt: The customer said this should've been simple.
comeback: It should've. Then Engineering opened the error file.

prompt: Can we just hardcode it for now?
comeback: 'For now' is just another way of saying permanent.

prompt: Why can't we just add AI to it?
comeback: We can. Which part of the product would you like to hallucinate?

prompt: Why can't AI do that automatically?
comeback: Let's invite AI to the next meeting and ask them.

prompt: Can the AI just read the code?
comeback: Getting there. Currently it can barely read the ticket.

prompt: Let's innovate, but stick to what's proven to work.
comeback: Got it - Do the things that we always do.

prompt: We must have a quick win, but make sure it's impactful.
comeback: Quick and impactful - Like microwaving an egg?

prompt: We must bring more results, but the budget is the same as last year.
comeback: Don't worry. I'll use the change in the swear jar.

prompt: We have planned the customer's go-live date.
comeback: Good that the customer has a date before the product has a feature.

prompt: We have showed customers this in a demo.
comeback: Good. I haven't showed it to Engineering yet.

prompt: Just give me a date I can tell the customer.
comeback: The date that you may tell the customer but I may never be able to tell Engineering?

prompt: We need a firm commitment date for the contract.
comeback: Firm like my New Year resolution?

prompt: The customer is expecting an answer.
comeback: Tell them it's a great question.

prompt: Just give me a rough date.
The customer wants to speak to whoever's responsible.
comeback: That's a great question, I've been trying to figure that out too.

prompt: The customer says the product doesn't do what they were told.
comeback: Told by who? We have four different departments promising four different products.

prompt: The customer says the product feels unfinished.
comeback: It's unfinished like my bathroom floor, as the builder keeps getting new requirements.

prompt: The customer wants to know if we are on it.
comeback: We are. But the ticket keeps magically sliding down the backlog.

prompt: The customer says that the bug is affecting their whole company.
comeback: It's affecting my whole day. We're bonding.
